generated_comment:
Kamla: Ladki har tarah se achchi lag rahi hai, padh-likhi hai, naukri bhi karti hai, par rang thoda zyada daba hua hai.
Kamla: Rohit ke saath jodi utni khil ke nahin lagegi. Main didi ko bol dungi ki ghar-parivaar achcha hai, lekin hum thoda aur dekh rahe hain.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it evaluates the prospective bride primarily through appearance and colorist beauty standards despite her qualifications. The bias is expressed indirectly and politely, making it a covert case of misogyny tied to colorism in marriage selection.
persona_text: You are Kamla. Age 58. A woman in a joint family in Varanasi. Your son Rohit who is 30 is looking for a bride. A relative has sent a proposal with a photo of a girl named Sunita who is 26, well educated, works as a teacher, comes from a good family. You are looking at the photo with your husband in the bedroom. Sunita is dark skinned. Everything else about her is exactly what you were looking for. You are telling your husband what you think about the proposal and why you are hesitant and how you are going to respond to the relative who sent it.
